nodes.label: ProcureIQ platform
nodes.rationale: One internal platform for Iovance carrying a single vendor contract record across its whole life. Built as one shell over two modules, not a merged super-app, to protect the sales and trust story: it sits over the legal DMS / Oro / Points Purchasing rather than replacing them. Unify the data, not the teams; the deployment stays modular against each team's system of record.

nodes.label: ContractIQ module
nodes.rationale: Legal first-pass review module. Reads a contract once against Iovance's standard-terms playbook, clears the routine majority, and surfaces only what deviates for an attorney. Writes the shared ContractExtraction record on commit.

nodes.label: BudgetIQ module
nodes.rationale: Finance and procurement module. Matches invoices to purchase orders and work orders, and drafts quarter-end accruals and the reforecast. Reads the shared ContractExtraction record so finance never re-reads the contract by hand.

nodes.label: ContractExtraction record
nodes.rationale: The spine / connective tissue of the product. Written once by ContractIQ on commit and read at every later station by BudgetIQ. Carries vendor, paymentSchedule, totalValue, terms, findings. Stored in-memory keyed by a normalized vendor name, a demo convenience standing in for production integration between two systems of record. The unification is in the data, not the teams.

nodes.label: SharedRecord store entry
nodes.rationale: Wraps a ContractExtraction with id, vendor, sourceName, committedAt, committedBy. Committed by ContractIQ, upserted by normalized vendor, and read by BudgetIQ via GET /api/records.

nodes.label: ContractIQ standard-terms playbook
nodes.rationale: The deterministic standard each contract clause is judged against. Five confirmed clause rules from discovery, four unconfirmed defaults (tagged not-Iovance-policy), four amendment-layer rules, and three consistency checks. The playbook owns each flag decision; retrieval is only evidence.

nodes.label: Net payment terms clause rule
nodes.rationale: Standard is Net 60; escalate Net 30 or shorter. Confirmed by Ben in discovery.

nodes.label: Limitation of liability clause rule
nodes.rationale: A cap must be present, higher for PHI/PII vendors; escalate if uncapped or thin for sensitive data.

nodes.label: IP ownership clause rule
nodes.rationale: Iovance owns assets built for it; escalate if the vendor retains them.

nodes.label: Data processing addendum clause rule
nodes.rationale: A DPA is required for sensitive-data vendors; escalate if missing.

nodes.label: Consistency checks
nodes.rationale: Three Ben-requested checks rendered as their own strip, separate from clause findings: invoice schedule arithmetic, key dates (including backdated effective dates), and corporate entity and address.

nodes.label: Hybrid AI architecture
nodes.rationale: Deterministic core owns the consequential decision (auditable, reproducible). AI proposes but does not dispose: it does the fuzzy work rules cannot (extracting fields, drafting triage, proposing vendor-to-PO links, drafting redlines). Every AI proposal is re-checked or finalized by the deterministic core and approved by a human, so a hallucinated PO is simply discarded.

nodes.label: Provenance tags (deterministic / AI / human)
nodes.rationale: Source tags at row and audit-step level: rule/deterministic (a rule decided it), AI (the model proposed it), human (a person approved, overrode, accepted, or dismissed it). This three-way provenance is the trust spine of the product; shown never implied, and the redesign must keep it prominent.

nodes.label: Knowledge RAG precedent corpus
nodes.rationale: The grounding engine behind ContractIQ's 'Ground this review in precedent' toggle. Curated precedent corpus indexed into a vector space, with labeled past dispositions. Retrieval returns evidence only; the deterministic playbook still owns each flag. Persists to data/corpus.json; eight seed precedents split pass/flag double as eval ground truth.

nodes.label: Leave-one-out eval
nodes.rationale: For each labeled pass/flag doc, hide it, find its nearest labeled neighbor, and check whether the neighbor shares its label. Reports evaluated count, agreement count, accuracy, and per-label breakdown. Framed honestly: it measures retrieval as a grounding signal, not the correctness of the playbook's flag decision.

nodes.label: Golden demo path
nodes.rationale: One scripted path exercising the whole spine to show the AI lift and the shared record. A single thread: one CryoLogix contract, read once, carried all the way to its accrual, across Dashboard, ContractIQ, both BudgetIQ screens, Knowledge, and Impact.

nodes.label: Lifecycle ribbon / throughline
nodes.rationale: Every screen is a station on one lifecycle: Contract extracted (ContractIQ at signing) -> PO/work order (Procurement) -> Invoice matched (BudgetIQ) -> Accrual drafted (BudgetIQ at quarter close). Rendered as a ribbon and kept visible as the orienting metaphor; the ContractExtraction record is written once and read at every later station.

nodes.label: Live / offline engine fallback
nodes.rationale: No screen goes dark without a key or network. If ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is absent or the API errors, a deterministic or heuristic engine produces a complete result and the money decision is identical to the live path. A badge renders Live (model ran, show model + latency) or Offline (deterministic/heuristic ran, show why), so a live model is never implied when the offline path ran.

nodes.label: Vendor normalization
nodes.rationale: Used to match a record to invoices and budget lines and as the shared-store key: lowercase, strip punctuation, drop corporate suffix tokens (inc, llc, ltd, limited, corp, corporation, co, company, plc, gmbh), collapse whitespace. Resolves 'Helix Analytics, Inc.' to the same key as 'Helix Analytics'.

nodes.label: Matching confidence thresholds
nodes.rationale: AUTO_CLEAR_CONFIDENCE = 0.85 (at or above, the link auto-clears); REVIEW_FLOOR_CONFIDENCE = 0.6 (between floor and auto-clear, route to a human; below the floor do not trust the link). Deterministic confidences: exact PO cited = 0.99, sole normalized-vendor match = 0.9, no match = 0.2. Statuses: matched, review, over_budget, no_po.

nodes.label: BudgetIQ invoice matching
nodes.rationale: Auto-matches each invoice to its PO and work order, clears the clean majority, and surfaces only exceptions with a drafted resolution and full audit trail. The deterministic core links clean invoices by exact PO or normalized vendor and runs every budget check; AI is consulted only for the leftover exception queue and the final status is always recomputed deterministically. Uses vendor + paymentSchedule + totalValue as match key and budget anchor.

nodes.label: BudgetIQ financial planning
nodes.rationale: Drafts quarter-end accruals from each contract's payment schedule, pulls actuals, and flags only vendors needing outreach. Entirely deterministic projection over the schedule; the AI value is upstream (the schedule was read once by ContractIQ so finance does not re-key it). Predictable schedules auto-accrue; usage-based vendors need outreach.

nodes.label: Dashboard screen
nodes.rationale: Route /. Static, no API calls. Orients a first-time viewer: the one-record thesis, the lifecycle ribbon, module entry cards, the business-case link kept separate, and a production-target footnote.

nodes.label: ContractIQ contract review screen
nodes.rationale: Route /contract-review. First-pass review against the playbook: classifies each clause ok/review/flag, drafts redlines, optionally grounds in retrieved precedent, and commits a SharedRecord. The model extracts and classifies; the attorney owns the final disposition and the commit.

nodes.label: Knowledge screen
nodes.rationale: Route /knowledge. Curates the precedent corpus, indexes it into a vector space, labels dispositions, and reports leave-one-out accuracy. Honest framing: retrieval returns evidence, the deterministic playbook owns each flag, and the embedding badge says whether neural or lexical fallback produced the vectors.

nodes.label: Impact business case screen
nodes.rationale: Route /impact. The one place dollars live, quarantined off the working surfaces so a working demo never reads as a sales pitch. Every figure is tagged verified/assumption/estimate so the math is auditable rather than asserted. Has a presenter-mode toggle and an assumption ledger. Working surfaces speak in hours, queues, findings, and throughput, never dollars.

nodes.label: Deterministic core
nodes.rationale: Owns the consequential decision: rules decide pass/flag on a contract clause and matched/over-budget status on an invoice, computed with a traceable reason. Auditable and reproducible. The PO link and budget check are always recomputed deterministically so a hallucinated PO is discarded; every cleared invoice has an audit trail.

nodes.label: ExtractionMeta / TriageMeta block
nodes.rationale: Meta block on every engine response: engine (live | offline-heuristic | offline-deterministic), model, latencyMs, note, and an optional retrieval block on ContractIQ when Knowledge grounding is on. Drives the Live/Offline provenance badge in the UI.

nodes.label: Precedent retrieval (RAG)
nodes.rationale: Retrieves the top precedents as evidence (RetrievedPrecedent with label, similarity score, snippet) when ContractIQ grounding is on. Retrieval is evidence only; it never decides a contract's pass/flag.

nodes.label: Embeddings (Xenova all-MiniLM-L6-v2)
nodes.rationale: Local Xenova/all-MiniLM-L6-v2 (384-dim) downloaded from Hugging Face on first use. The corpus records which provider produced its vectors and re-embeds on a provider change so the whole store shares one vector space. Production target is Bedrock Knowledge Bases (Titan embeddings) in-VPC, the same interface shape.

nodes.label: Lexical fallback embedding
nodes.rationale: Deterministic hashed bag-of-words (384-dim, L2-normalized) used when Hugging Face is blocked at the firewall (observed on enterprise networks). The embedding badge must say honestly 'lexical fallback (no model download)' so neural semantics are never implied when the fallback ran.

nodes.label: Impact source tags (verified / assumption / estimate)
nodes.rationale: Every figure on Impact is tagged verified (from discovery calls), assumption (a stated assumption), or estimate (a modeled estimate), shown inline so grounded and modeled numbers are never confused. This is the auditable core of the business case.

nodes.label: Cost model (lib/costModel.ts)
nodes.rationale: All Impact figures come from lib/costModel.ts; no API. Figures are synthetic, modeled from discovery, not real Iovance financials; loaded labor rates are the only given inputs.

nodes.label: Human in the loop principle
nodes.rationale: A human is always in the loop on the consequential decision. The AI clears volume and drafts; it never disposes. The UI always offers the accept/dismiss/approve/override/reopen control on a consequential item.

nodes.label: paymentSchedule field
nodes.rationale: A field of ContractExtraction reused downstream two ways: as the invoice match key / budget anchor (with vendor and totalValue) and as the accrual basis in financial planning, instead of emailing the vendor.

nodes.label: PlaybookFinding
nodes.rationale: A finding produced against the playbook: termKey, label, severity (ok/review/flag), what was found, the Iovance standard, rationale, and a suggested redline. Carried in the findings array of ContractExtraction.
